source_reviews.notes: The official 2022 NAICS page confirms that 238220 covers plumbing, heating, and air-conditioning installation and service and includes HVAC, mechanical, refrigeration, sprinkler, and plumbing examples; this supports the enumerated operating identities without supplying target economics.

The reopened official EC2223KOB file reports 238220 total receipts of 297,608,835 thousand dollars, HVAC receipts of 121,737,865, plumbing receipts of 75,077,588, and mechanical-contractor receipts of 54,364,599; the resulting shares reproduce rounded bases 471, 291, and 210 from n_band 1,152.
MCAA describes member firms spanning heating, air conditioning, refrigeration, plumbing, piping, mechanical systems, and HVACR service, supporting the distinct integrated-mechanical alternative only.
ACCA describes professionals installing and maintaining HVACR and related systems, supporting the selected HVAC-focused operating identity without supporting its firm-count endpoints.
PHCC describes plumbing and HVACR contractor businesses across construction and service segments, supporting the plumbing-focused alternative's operating context only.

The official table is the same EC2223KOB endpoint reopened through its downloadable file; it supports the all-size activity shares and the disclosed proxy arithmetic, not direct firm counts by operating identity.

The official SUSB page confirms that the dataset tabulates firms, employment, payroll, and receipts by industry and enterprise employment size. Its independent role is limited to the frozen size-band denominator context; it does not corroborate HVAC operating identity, as the packet candidly states.
The BLS page confirms HVAC mechanics and installers as an occupation, but the packet correctly does not use it for role weights, cash costs, or removability.

input_reviews.notes: Official Census receipts reproduce the objective base ordering: HVAC 471 exceeds plumbing 291 and mechanical 210; the three bases total 972, the named residual is 180, and the overlapping HVAC/plumbing ranges correctly force one-record selection uncertainty and UNPROVEN readiness.
The frozen value is preserved pointwise at 1,152 and remains an ESTIMATE/ASSUMPTION with NONE quality; it is not promoted by the packet sources.
All values are candidly null/MISSING; the closed accounting basis is ACCRUAL_RECOGNIZED_REVENUE over TRAILING_TWELVE_MONTHS with GROSS_OF_ALL presentation.
GROSS_OF_ALL matches recognized revenue; both disjoint partitions are empty and exactly cover the empty pass-through set. The construct remains MISSING because no target documentation exists.
No pass-through item is invented or deducted; the empty array is consistent with the missing documentation and cannot overlap either numerator cost ID.
The 12.0/17.5/23.0 million-dollar sizing is bounded, source-free ESTIMATE/ASSUMPTION/NONE exactly as the frozen assumption basis requires and is not presented as measured payroll.
The 0/1.5/3.0 million-dollar sizing is bounded, source-free ESTIMATE/ASSUMPTION/NONE, uses a distinct cost ID, and excludes any deductible pass-through.
All six frozen role titles and weights are unchanged, sum to 1.00, remain ESTIMATE/ASSUMPTION/NONE with empty sources, and do not substitute the broader normalized occupation mix.

Values exactly equal 353/1,152, 471/1,152, and 589/1,152. The all-size-revenue-to-size-band-firm-count limitation is explicit; S2 independently supports only denominator context and is disjoint from S1, not evidence of HVAC identity.
Availability remains null/MISSING and is not inferred from ownership age, access, price, or capacity.

Capacity remains null/MISSING while preserving DEAL_EXECUTION, the closed five-year horizon, MAXIMUM_TARGET_COUNT, NON_DUPLICATIVE_TARGET_COUNT, and exact buyer/geography/archetype/onboarding scope.
Capacity remains null/MISSING while preserving INTEGRATION_ONBOARDING and the identical closed horizon, count calculation, and scope without collapsing it into deal execution.
Paid entry multiple remains null/MISSING because there are no closed all-in selected-archetype comparables; price is not substituted into buyability.
All six dimensions and the canonical digest remain null/MISSING, with the required entry-reference, one-state scenario policy, and no-sponsor-transformation policy preserved.

The anchor and digest are null/MISSING consistently; adjustments are empty, the required entry-equivalent anchor channel remains declared, and every excluded deterioration or transformation channel is closed out.

The basket is correctly MISSING with no services because no exhaustive baseline ledger reconciles to R_cva. All required fixed-base Laspeyres, constant-price, constant-quality, baseline-only, named-operator, no-new-demand, and pass-through-boundary assertions remain intact.

publication_caveats: This record applies only to the frozen HVAC-focused contractor archetype; its count range overlaps the plumbing alternative and does not support a NAICS-wide conclusion.

Evidence readiness is UNPROVEN: the value-basis amounts and removability fractions are source-free assumptions, while the score-driving operating, commercial, transaction, valuation, and survival primitives are missing.

The record is economically useful as a disciplined evidence-gap map only; its low-to-high sensitivity spans kill to hell_yes and cannot support underwriting, prioritization, or an acquisition decision before target evidence is supplied.
summary: Publishable with caveats. Frozen identity, digests, schemas, arithmetic, deterministic finalization, memo rendering, causal separation, missing-value treatment, readiness, sensitivity, and action all check out. The official Census endpoints reproduce the objective archetype arithmetic, the assumption-mode value basis remains candidly source-free, and unsupported economics are not imputed. Publication must preserve the one-archetype scope and present the result only as an UNPROVEN evidence-first record.
